description_long: This is a two-story rectangular house with a simple, utilitarian design, clad in white siding and topped with a medium-pitch gable roof made of metal or similar material. The front features a small covered porch with steps leading up to a single main door, and a modest awning provides shelter above the entry. The sides of the house have evenly spaced, vertically oriented windows with minimal trim, and the rear includes a larger covered porch area supported by posts. The house sits on a red brick foundation, and the surrounding area is a neatly maintained grassy lawn. The overall appearance suggests a practical, early 20th-century American vernacular style, with little ornamentation and a focus on function.
description: A two-story, white-sided house with a gable roof, brick foundation, covered porches, and simple vertical windows, set on a grassy lot.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entry with a small covered porch, steps, and a single door.
description_view_1: Side view displaying three evenly spaced windows, a brick foundation, and a covered rear porch extending from the back.
description_view_2: Rear view highlighting a larger covered porch, two doors, and a set of steps leading to the backyard.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with three windows, a brick foundation, and a partial view of the covered porch.
attribute_entryway_design: Single front door with small covered porch, steps, and a simple awning
